other_observers: Mark Peterson and I found the bird; Christian Nunes and some from his DFO (?) group also saw it briefly
description: First seen in flight. Resembled a Turkey Vulture, and I initially called it that until seeing the finely barred tail. Mark Peterson shouted 'Common Black Hawk', which clued me in to the fact that this was not a TUVU. I also realized that COBH was not right either, because this bird had a finely barred tail (obvious in flight), and finely barred black and white remiges, but was otherwise dark overall, including the upperwing (visible when the bird banked north and briefly disappeared from sight). In flight, it also showed the typical characteristic "tilting" glide with wings in a V shape (a la TUVU, which gave me the initial inpression), and unlike COBH which soars/glides on flat wings with tails spread. We refound the bird perched nearby, and Mark obtained photos. Perched showed all dark plumage, massive yellow feet, yellow cere (though bill not quite as heavy or long as COBH and lacking yellow on lores), and white speckling on breast
